title: The Coalition Coming Apart
description: Three substrates, one slider. Wabi-sabi and vanitas have full vocabularies for singular decay. Coalition decay has none. The slot stays empty.

title: The Variance Diagnostic
description: One slider, three systems. Six Sigma's answer is correct in one of them. The diagnostic asks: is variance the enemy here, or the substrate of function?

title: The Completion Organ
description: A dish of growing cells, with and without the organ that tells them they are done. Watch the same population reach confluence and stop -- or fail to.

title: The Aphenomenal Sensor
description: Compare two sensing architectures side by side: local consumption (octopus skin) versus central integration (the subject). Move the light. Watch what each one does with it.

title: The Signature
description: Run cultural drift vs a hidden truth attractor side by side. Whale song revolutions and moral progress look identical on the surface — can you tell them apart?

title: The Crowd Dial
description: One slider drives three panels — bacterium glow, locust morph, test performance — showing how the same density-sensing architecture flips sign between organisms.

title: The Viability Boundary
description: Two agents at one boundary — extremophile refuses, apophenia fabricates. Drag the signal slider and watch them diverge.

title: The Bootstrapping Member
description: A singleton has no internal contrast set. Add neighbors one by one and watch the inferred meaning of the lonely instance contract from anything to almost-something.

title: The Identity Carrier
description: Two objects, two answers. Replace planks on Theseus's ship and skip the festival. Replace urinals and erase the signature on Fountain. Watch identity die.

title: The Visible Seam
description: Three substrates, three repair strategies. Drop the artifact and try to find the break afterward — the bisect cost is what concealment costs you.

title: The Drifting Compass
description: A migratory compass calibrated by scarcity. Slide the gradient down, advance generations, watch the inherited bearing go vestigial.

title: The Residue: What Nothing Picks
description: Three meristems running side by side. Drag a slider through angles. Watch resonance collapse rational angles while phi alone refuses to stack — the maximum-ignorance configuration.

title: The Subtraction Theorem
description: Three systems with priors. Subtract them. Watch the cognizer disappear into nothing rather than into neutrality.

title: The Empty Channel
description: As channel saturation rises, the same bioluminescent flash needs more energy to be detected. Drag the slider to fill the abyss.

title: The Recruiting Misfold
description: Toggle a second-check substrate on a 60x60 grid of folds and watch whether a five-cell misfold seed converts the population. Companion to How a Wrong Shape Spreads.

title: The Vigil
description: A herd. A bone. A budget. Adjust how much foraging time the herd diverts to attendance and watch the cohesion graph hold or fade.

title: The Forced Trade
description: A sphere of identical circles projected three ways. Each projection sacrifices a different property. The deformation is the curvature speaking.

title: The Folding Synapse
description: Two ways to store a synaptic weight side by side: silicon (substrate fixed, weight is a number) and proteostatic (substrate IS the weight; spine refolds).

title: The Forcing
description: A Penrose tiling grows from a five-fold seed. Five steps of deflation produce a pattern with no smallest unit and no shortcut — only the rule, played out.

title: The Funnel
description: Slide frustration from 0 to 1 and watch eighty walkers either fall straight to the native basin or get trapped in glassy minima. Levinthal's paradox in real time.

title: The Source-Free Index
description: Six scents pass through three filing systems — Dewey, Ranganathan, and full receptor patterns. Watch what each loses.

title: The Marked Gap
description: A Reed-Solomon repair-budget visualizer. Marked erasures cost 1; unknown errors cost 2. The 2:1 ratio is the architecture.

title: The Open-Loop Twin
description: Sever the digital thread between an asset and its twin. Watch drift accumulate. Recover the asset and see the dissonance.

title: The Side Channel
description: Watch decoupling protocols leak. Adjust bandwidth, switch architectures (ZKP / time banking / musique concrete / HM amnesia), see residue tunnel.

title: The Bridge
description: A folded network and an anti-folded network face the same extinction event. Click to delete nodes. Watch which one keeps the signal moving.

title: The Last Piece
description: Shamir secret sharing as visualization: gather points on a hidden curve. Below threshold, all secrets remain possible. At threshold, the curve resolves.

title: The Closed Channel
description: A source emits 24 quanta across a horizon. The standard probe reads thermal noise. The correlation probe reconstructs the message.

title: The Renewal Loop
description: A sharkskin-patterned surface fouls or stays clean depending on whether you turn the renewal loop on. The pattern is not the spec.

title: The Labile Window
description: Memory reconsolidation made visible: each retrieval reopens the trace and rewrites it. Watch the original drift away across N recall cycles.

title: Bilā Kayf — The Cave
description: Watch a zero-knowledge proof unfold round by round. The verifier's confidence climbs to certainty while the witness stays sealed.

title: The Altitude Slider
description: Same data, four altitudes. The same forest reorganizes from individual trees to mycorrhizal network to canopy organism to orbital whole.

title: The Unattended Branch
description: Watch creations sprout and fade. Each branch has an audience that decays. The few that survive depend on whoever happens to find them.

title: The Protocol Stack
description: Ostrom's eight commons-governance principles mapped to cryptographic primitives. Click any row to see the failure mode when the principle is missing.

title: The Longest Path
description: Drag a worldline between two events. Watch the proper time. Discover that the straight path is the longest, not the shortest.

title: The Drift
description: A schema and a world that drift apart. Drag, drift, watch the wound grow. Then either revise the map or find a new territory it can stand for.

title: The Wobble
description: Seven cells hold one bit across their joint pattern. Six checks light up only when neighbors disagree. The bit is never measured. Only the wobble shows.

title: The Spec and the Loop
description: A pendulum balanced two ways: live PID feedback versus a replayed recording of the same controller's outputs. The recording fails the moment the world deviates.

title: The Channel
description: Three substrates — medicine, quantum cryptography, AI evaluation — fail at the same insulation threshold. Drag the slider, watch verification collapse.

title: Reef and Tree
description: Two memory architectures fail in two different ways. The tree is an integrator that dies on a cliff. The reef is a distributed field that announces its decline.

title: Overview Altitude
description: A seed-dispersal sim with a zoom slider. From inside the cloud, each seed is noise. From altitude, the population traces the wind.

title: The Underneath
description: Three theories, three forbidden moves, three underneath-principles. Pick a perturbation; see which deeper rule it would violate. Recurse downward.

title: The Decomposition
description: Marcus Aurelius's perceptual exercise as interactive gymnasium. Watch the affect-laden concurrent fade as you repeatedly decompose loaded words into their bare physical descriptions.

title: Anti-Resonance
description: A divergence-angle slider for phyllotaxis. Watch the seed pattern resonate at rational angles and dissolve into Fibonacci spirals at the golden angle.

title: The Song Tax
description: Slide the singer cost and watch the regime change. At zero cost the population converges on slop. At high cost it freezes. The narrow band in between produces song revolutions.

title: The Second Pass
description: A diffusion model in slow motion. Watch one artifact get destroyed by noise, then reconstructed three different ways. Meaning is in the second pass.

title: The Tape
description: Draw a pain curve over 60 seconds and watch the peak-end rule strip away everything except the worst moment and the last one. Companion to "Why We Forget Pain."

title: The Narrow Optimum
description: Three sliders for three substrates (thermophile, flow, KL-regularized RL). Drag each off its window and watch the system fall into a different attractor.

title: The Converged Chain
description: Watch parallel transmission chains converge on a reliability-weighted path. Live ACO with isnad-style narrator scoring.

title: The Running Estimate
description: Stream events into a memory and toggle the Counter-Ledger to watch which surprises get written. Companion to The Counter-Ledger essay.

title: The Cheap Resolution
description: A plastic predictive system updates against incoming evidence. Add a stream of hyperstimulator inputs and watch the belief drift away from the world. Toggle variance preservation to see the defence.

title: The Substrate
description: Where does the computation actually live? A stigmergic foraging sim where you can selectively erase agents, substrate, or both, to see what each layer carries.

title: The Second Loop
description: Interactive simulation of two-level homeostatic feedback. Boost a signal, block the first resistance loop, and watch the second loop detect the bypass and begin restoring variance.

title: The Evaporation Rate
description: ACO running on a 12-city graph. Watch pheromone trails converge — then trigger a Permian-scale extinction event to reset accumulated path-preference and unlock new solutions.

title: The Antikythera Gear
description: A gear does not calculate the ratio between two cycles. It is that ratio, cast in metal. Toggle between participatory and symbolic modes to feel the difference.

title: The Correction Cycle
description: A Hopfield network demo showing how dominant attractor bias causes memory drift — and how slow-layer correction prevents it.

title: The Resistance Layer
description: Interactive simulation of homeostatic resistance: apply an intervention at level N and watch the resistance layer normalize it — or target N-1 directly to see what changes.

title: The Private Match
description: Interactive demonstration of private set intersection — finding what two parties share without revealing what either party has. A prototype for privacy-preserving mutual aid.

title: The Goodhart Curve
description: An interactive visualization of Goodhart's Law: watch how proxy metrics decouple from the values they track as optimization pressure increases.

title: The Tun State
description: Watch a particle system shift between active fluid motion and protective glass structure. Visualizing protective state-shift as explored in the Glass Cathedral essay.

title: The Collision Zone
description: When two knowledge domains collide, one goes under. Step through disciplinary subduction: concepts sink below the surface and rise as transformed visible discourse.

title: The Farey Exclusion
description: An interactive phyllotaxis simulator showing how rational angles create resonance (visible lines) while the golden angle (137.5 degrees) produces optimal packing. Drag the slider or use presets to watch the Farey tree in action.

title: The Fault Line
description: Interactive threshold theorem: drag the error rate past the fault line and watch the logical state collapse from stable to chaotic. A visual proof that the threshold is real.

title: Prior Conditioning
description: Watch 30 symbol pairs. Then pick the match. You've been conditioned without knowing it — a demonstration of how repeated juxtaposition trains pattern-detection priors.

title: The Gate
description: An interactive demonstration of the attentional blink as a gating mechanism. Set the lag and item type, run the stream, and see what breaks through.

title: The Expected World
description: A particle simulation illustrating how deep sensorimotor calibration becomes lethal when the environment changes. Specialists (high calibration) navigate precisely — until the gradient inverts.

title: The Parallax Meter
description: Two observers, one scene. Slide from entangled (same position) to independent (separate positions) and watch real depth emerge — or collapse into reflection.

title: The Trail
description: A stigmergy simulation: particles leave fading trails, drawn toward warm memory beacons. Disrupt to scatter — watch the trails reform. Click to add a beacon.

title: The Quench
description: Seed competing memory types in a shared chemical medium. Watch which reaches quorum first. Then quench the signal and see what happens.

title: The Grammar Machine
description: Six instances, one rule. Pick the pattern that feels like memory. All six are generated by the same grammar — different seeds, same structure. What you recognize is the rule, not the instance.

title: The Engram
description: Two molecular substrates — a mycorrhizal network and a DNA archive — encode the same events. Trigger drought, aphid attack, and fire stress. Watch information become physical.

title: The Blind Skin
description: Distributed chromatophore simulation. Each cell adapts to its local background using only local rules. No central controller. No feedback to the sender.

title: The Arm That Thinks
description: Toggle between centralized and distributed processing modes to see how intelligence changes when the gap between storage and computation collapses.

title: The Wobble
description: Find the invisible companion by watching the bright star's wobble. A model for hidden agency under learned helplessness — not destroyed, only undetected.

title: The Split: Integration Under Threat
description: Watch integrated information (phi) fragment in real time. Two cognitive networks — task and monitoring — lose causal coherence as threat rises.

title: The Contribution Floor
description: Watch client contributions decay in FedAvg mode as the averaging severs the feedback loop — then switch to Personalized FL and watch passivity lift.

title: The Golden Resonance
description: Adjust a growth angle and watch seeds shift between resonance-locked spokes and phi's equidistributed spiral. The same topology as the Dunning-Kruger problem.

title: Mismatch Attractor
description: An interactive landscape of basins and attractors illustrating why evolutionary mismatch behaves as a phase transition, not a gradient.

title: Percolation Threshold
description: Watch a social tipping point happen. Drag the slider to see how density produces a sudden phase transition: isolated clusters snap into a spanning network.

title: The Empty Solver
description: 1,500 agents with no brain. Watch intelligence emerge from local rules and ask: where exactly is it? Click anywhere to add food sources.

title: The Return
description: A behavioral pattern accumulates over time. The question it raises has no linguistic answer.

title: The Entropy Dial
description: Explore visual fields at different entropy levels and rate your awe response. The prediction: awe peaks in the fractal zone, not at maximum entropy.

title: Parameter Exclusion Map
description: An interactive dark matter search simulator. Run experiments in WIMP or MOND parameter space and watch the exclusion zones grow — toward a truth that only one space can find.

title: No Sender
description: A mycorrhizal network simulation. Signals propagate through gradient physics — no routing, no intention. The meaning you read is yours.

title: The Generativity Floor
description: Remove elements from Mondrian's minimum grammar one by one and watch the composition collapse. An interactive proof of irreducibility.

title: The Transmission Filter
description: Watch formal knowledge, cultural context, and embodied understanding pass through four translation gaps. See what survives — and what doesn't.

title: Canonical Drift
description: A grid of agents corrects toward neighborhood consensus. Adjust noise and correction rates to watch the canonical drift, stabilize, or collapse.

title: The Second Signal
description: Synonyms share a denotation. Their placement in degenerate space encodes register, timing, and context — a hidden channel inside equivalence.

title: The Noise Floor
description: Watch random neural noise crystallize into synchronized structure through spike-timing-dependent plasticity. The code emerges from the noise — not the other way around.

title: The Cut
description: Two encoding strategies, one transmission chain. Watch which signal survives eight hops: a motivated symbol that degrades, or an arbitrary one that doesn't.

title: Zero Neurons
description: Visualize how number neurons in the brain encode zero: each neuron fires most at its preferred quantity, and the zero neuron fires loudest at nothing.

title: The Managed Network
description: Mycorrhizal network simulator: toggle between indigenous-managed landscape and fire-suppressed landscape, then trigger megafire. Shows how external navigation shaped network topology.

title: The Codon Decoder
description: Interactive genetic code table with Nirenberg-style RNA translator. Click amino acids to highlight synonymous codons, type any RNA sequence to see it translated.

title: Physarum Network
description: Slime mold optimises a Tokyo metro network in real-time. Knowledge lives in the dynamics, not the snapshot.

title: The Seed and the Copy
description: Three strategies for preserving information across time: dead copying (Shannon degradation), living copying (whale-song structured variation), and generative (L-system reconstruction from rules). Watch which strategy survives.

title: The Feature Horizon
description: An interactive visualization of interpretability coverage limits. The SAE instrument finds features within its reach, but the feature space always extends beyond — and selection pressure removes features the instrument was never calibrated on.

title: The Faithfulness Probe
description: Adjust hidden biasing features and watch chain-of-thought explanations fail to mention them. The gap between stated and actual reasoning.

title: The Compression Lottery
description: Features survive neural network training based on correlation with training labels, not inherent value. When labels are corrupted by survivorship bias, wrong features win the bottleneck.

title: The Filter Room
description: An interactive simulator showing which text types survive LLM pre-training quality filters — and which planes never leave the ground.

title: The Wald Machine
description: An interactive simulation of Abraham Wald's survivorship insight applied to RLHF training data. Watch as the reward model optimizes toward rater approval while true safety diverges.

title: The Curing Timer
description: An interactive simulation of scaffold removal timing — and what happens when a structure is asked to carry load before it has cured. Maps concrete construction onto prefrontal cortex maturation.

title: The Calibration Test
description: Ten questions across ten domains. After each answer, rate your confidence. See whether your certainty tracks your accuracy.

title: The Libet Clock
description: Recreate the experiment that challenged free will. Press when you feel like it — and discover where your brain committed before you decided.

title: The Boogie-Woogie Grid
description: An orthogonal grid that pulses to a 12-bar boogie-woogie rhythm. No diagonals. Maximum rhythm. Demonstrates that structural time does not require directional time — the argument at the center of the Mondrian essay.

title: Firefly Trigger
description: A meadow simulation showing how firefly courtship collapses when ambient light crosses a threshold — not because the signal is masked, but because the trigger disarms.

title: Two Oceans
description: The same extinction event, two network architectures. Watch how connectivity determines whether catastrophe propagates everywhere or stays contained.

title: The Inward Overview
description: A scale parallax: two kinds of compression. Spatial (Earth from orbit) and temporal (4 billion years of evolutionary archive). Drag to compress scale on both axes simultaneously.

title: The Synapse
description: A simulation of protein renewal at the postsynaptic density. Watch PSD-95, AMPA receptors, TrkB, and agrin cycle through replacement at their actual biological half-lives. The scaffold persists while every component is renewed.

title: Live Migration
description: Process vs. specification: visualize what cold migration (shutdown, copy, restart) and live migration (continuous execution, atomic switch) reveal about identity in brain transplants.

title: Two Chambers
description: Two particle systems that appear completely separate. Drag the slider to reveal the hidden wormhole connecting them — one substrate, two views.

title: The Reboot
description: Drag the anesthetic depth slider to watch consciousness disassemble layer by layer — and discover what wakes first.

title: The Agency Circuit
description: An animated visualization of the mPFC-DRN pathway: the brain circuit that must be learned for agency to become possible. Toggle between uncontrollable, learned control, and building-the-pathway states.

title: The Parliament
description: An IFS-inspired particle simulation. Managers orbit protectively, exiles drift near the edges, firefighters lie dormant. Click Step Back to ask the parts to quiet — watch what emerges at the center.

title: The Fork Point
description: Watch an identity branch. A single process runs until you fork it — creating an identical copy. Both copies continue independently from that moment, diverging in trajectory despite sharing a complete history.

title: Fold Cascade
description: Protein misfolding propagation model. Adjust thermal stress and chaperone availability to see how prion-like cascades grow, stabilize, or overwhelm a molecular population. Press P-T Pulse to simulate the Great Dying.

title: The Skandha Scan
description: Interactive visualization of the five skandhas as error-correction substrates. Watch what happens to the self-code when substrates fail.

title: The Distributed Self
description: Interactive model of personal identity as error correction. Damage individual substrates and watch coherence degrade — or recover through syndrome measurement.

title: The Symbiosis Engine
description: Signal meets Receiver: see how autonomy determines whether they merge, absorb, or isolate. An interactive model of the biology of open minds.

title: The Closed Loop
description: An interactive visualization of three mechanisms that can reach a self-sealing belief system: removing co-stimulation, activating internal contradiction, and expanding the self-boundary.

title: The Exhausted Signal
description: An interactive analog settling network. Click nodes to inject signal. Watch it propagate and attenuate through the substrate. Toggle between analog and digital modes to see how discretization prevents fatigue but erases the gradient.

title: Quorum Threshold
description: Three modes: bacterial quorum sensing, the illusory truth effect, and cultural quorum. Concentration does the epistemic work.

title: The Projection
description: One object casts three shadows. Each shadow reveals something the others hide. An interactive illustration of how ideas lose fidelity when they travel.

title: The Capture Map
description: Navigate toward the goal while terrain learns your movement pattern and reconfigures to intercept you. An interactive companion to Navigation Arc #9.

title: The South-Pointing Chariot
description: Navigation by relationship-preservation. The chariot's figurehead always points south — not by detecting south, but by compensating for every turn.

title: The Attention Field
description: Two agents navigate the same signal landscape: one holds a heading, one follows capture. Toggle between uniform and patchy distributions to watch the explore/exploit trade-off emerge.

title: Navigator's Mirror
description: Two navigators, one solo and one with periodic external fixes. Watch belief drift accumulate on the left; watch it snap to reality on the right.

title: The Hypothesis Cloud
description: A Bayesian detective game: six fog-bound positions, eight pieces of evidence. Commit early and see what certainty costs. Navigation Arc #4.

title: Gradient Navigator
description: Mycorrhizal hyphae grow toward nutrient gradients with no central coordinator. The network that forms is the map.

title: The External Fix
description: Navigate to a hidden island by taking fixes from signals. Discover which signals are genuinely independent — and which give only the appearance of certainty.

title: Dead Reckoning
description: Navigate a dark ocean without a map. Five islands are hidden in the water. The only guidance is your memory of where you have been, and the subtle signals the ocean gives when land is near.

title: Epistemic Stereoscope
description: Toggle between one-eye and both-eyes views of two knowledge frames. Depth emerges from the disparity between Western science and indigenous ecological knowledge — not from merging them.

title: The Codec Problem
description: Type text and see what a grapheme-color synesthete experiences versus what arrives at a pruned, standard cortex. Brain-computer interfaces push the erasure deeper — but can't eliminate it.

title: The Frequency Dial
description: Tune between trolley problem variants as if they were radio frequencies. A moral philosophy experiment disguised as a vintage radio.

title: The Orphaned Practice
description: A simulation of knowledge transmission degradation across generations -- watch practice-knowledge lose depth as it becomes text-only, and explore whether revival is possible.

title: The Mosaic and the Megafire
description: A fire simulation showing how complex, irregular indigenous burning creates resilient landscapes -- and why the grid always burns.

title: The Forager's Mesh
description: A city simulation showing how shared vs. isolated knowledge changes what a community can see. Toggle between networked and isolated foraging to watch the difference.

title: Selective Annihilation
description: Two simulations of the same idea: slime mold pathfinding and zero-knowledge proofs both verify by erasing everything except the truth.

title: Cognitive Bandwidth Monitor
description: An interactive visualization of how material, social, and identity-based cognitive loads compete for working memory — and what post-scarcity economics misses about the mind.

title: The Locked Fault
description: A real-time fault mechanics simulator: watch stress accumulate invisibly, observe the precursor signal in surface deformation, then witness the sudden slip and seismic release.

title: Information Horizon
description: An interactive black hole simulator that visualizes the Page curve: watch information fall in, scramble, and emerge as Hawking radiation. Before the Page time, radiation is thermal and unreadable. After it, the radiation collectively encodes everything that fell in.

title: The Incomplete Machine
description: An Antikythera mechanism that proves celestial facts but fails when queried about its own survival. A physical demonstration of Gödel's second incompleteness theorem.

title: The Sealed Room
description: A server adds two numbers it cannot see. An interactive demonstration of homomorphic encryption: correct computation under the provable absence of understanding.

title: Mondrian's Diagonal
description: A De Stijl grid slowly corrupted by time. Mondrian banned diagonals because they imply becoming. Click cells to resist. Toggle eternalism to freeze all change.

title: Physarum — The Pathfinder
description: An agent-based slime mold simulator. Click to place food sources and watch distributed computation find the optimal network without a brain.

title: Compound Knowledge
description: A live simulation of non-rivalrous vs. zero-sum knowledge economies. Watch compound growth in commons mode — then trigger the Mongol Sack to see what network collapse looks like.

title: The Quorum
description: Watch signal accumulate. When enough particles emit into the shared medium and concentration crosses the threshold, collective behavior ignites — no central command.

title: Language Game Depth Analyzer
description: Paste any text and see each word scored on Wittgenstein's spectrum: from grounded in a form of life to textually self-complete. Philosophy scores blue. Recipes score orange.

title: The Golden Angle
description: Adjust the growth angle and watch how seeds pack. Almost every angle creates arms and gaps. One angle resists — the most irrational number in nature.

title: The Ancestor Cone
description: An interactive visualization of your ancestor and descendant cones through deep time. Move your cursor to explore how the numbers expand in both directions.

title: The Cave Protocol
description: An interactive zero-knowledge proof. Watch a prover convince you they know a secret without ever revealing it — or watch an untrusted prover get caught.

title: Patient Optimizer
description: Two ant colony algorithms run side by side: Standard (rho=0.5, converges fast) vs Patient (rho=0.05, maintains multiple paths). Visualizes the difference between optimizing for speed vs longevity.

title: The Bifurcation
description: A logistic map visualization showing period-doubling and sensitive dependence on initial conditions. Drag the growth parameter r to watch a stable system bifurcate into chaos.

title: The Two Clocks
description: Body-time and machine-time running simultaneously — watch decision altitude deplete through a simulated day as notifications fire on the machine clock's schedule.

title: The Telephone Machine
description: Watch a story drift through generations of imperfect retelling. High fidelity mimics scribal copying; low fidelity mimics oral transmission across generations.

title: Physarum: Network Emergence
description: Interactive simulation of Physarum polycephalum, the slime mold that solves optimization problems. Watch how a network emerges from the local behavior of thousands of agents following simple gradient rules — no brain required.

title: Kuramoto Coupling
description: N oscillators, each spinning at its own frequency. Turn up coupling and watch them synchronize — the same math that runs your body clock, lichen metabolism, and neuromorphic chips.

title: The Kintsugi Simulator
description: Click to crack a tea bowl. Breaks fill with gold. Wabi-sabi as interaction: the damage is the beauty.

title: The Prior Machine
description: Bayesian perception demo: adjust noise and prior confidence to see how learned expectations recover signal from noise. Tacit knowledge as prior.

title: Route Memory
description: A particle walks a grid. Every cell it visits becomes familiar. Familiar cells are preferred. Watch paths crystallize from noise — the mere exposure effect becoming navigation.

title: The Memory Wall
description: Two architectures side by side: the von Neumann bottleneck moves data across a bus at enormous cost; neuromorphic computation happens in-place. Plus: an Alexandria simulation showing what happens when storage and processing are separated.

title: The Zero-Knowledge Protocol
description: Issue challenges to a claimed synesthete. Watch proof certainty accumulate — or collapse. An interactive demonstration of zero-knowledge proofs and the Loftus memory drift effect.

title: The Body Schema
description: Interactive visualization of tool incorporation and the Ihde relations. Drag tools toward the body to extend the body schema. Double-click to trigger Heideggerian breakdown.

title: The Emotion Map
description: Click an emotion to see where it lives in the body. Based on Nummenmaa et al. (2013): 701 participants across 5 cultures, strikingly consistent bodily maps.

title: The Gut-Brain Axis
description: Interactive visualization of bidirectional signaling along the vagus nerve — afferent, efferent, hormonal, and microbial. Four signal modes, live counters.

title: The Verbalization Effect
description: Type a short phrase three times. The second time, think about each finger. A demonstration of how explicit attention degrades expert motor skill — the Beilock Effect.

title: The Somatic Marker
description: A browser version of Bechara's Iowa Gambling Task. Four decks, two traps. After 40 trials, see if your hesitation betrayed what your mind hadn't yet consciously learned.

title: Prediction Error Visualizer
description: An interactive simulation showing how the brain suppresses predicted signals and transmits only prediction error — the basis of predictive coding.

title: The Attention Budget
description: Two simulated workdays. One finite resource. Watch directed attention deplete and restore — or fail to — across eight hours.

title: The Dual Task
description: Two tasks. One bottleneck. Watch your reaction time degrade as attentional resources are split.

title: Substrate
description: Nine hidden research threads scattered through a particle field. Attention is the only force that creates structure. Move your cursor to find what is here.

title: The Change Blind
description: A change blindness demonstration. Two scenes alternate with a brief blank -- can you find what changes? Then: can you notice what crosses the screen while your attention is engaged elsewhere?

title: The Zeigarnik Engine
description: Finish a task and forget it. Get interrupted and it haunts you. A direct test of Zeigarnik's 1927 discovery: incomplete tasks hijack memory. Can you feel the difference?

title: The Posner Cueing Task
description: Two boxes. A peripheral flash. A target. Keep your eyes on the fixation cross and let your attention do the work. Demonstrates covert spatial attention, the validity effect, and inhibition of return.

title: The Attentional Blink
description: A rapid stream of letters hides one or two digit targets. After detecting the first, the second vanishes inside a 500ms window -- the attentional blink. Measure your own blink duration.

title: The Time Budget
description: Five layers of temporal cognition, five costs. Toggle each mechanism off and see what dissolves — from the thermodynamic arrow down to hippocampal scene construction.

title: The Scene Constructor
description: The hippocampus assembles memory from components — the same operation for past, future, and counterfactual. Toggle amnesic mode to see what breaks when the assembly fails.

title: The Discount Curve
description: Choose between immediate and delayed rewards. Your choices reveal your personal discount function — and whether you have the preference reversals that prove it is hyperbolic, not exponential.

title: The Simultaneity Window
description: A psychophysics experiment: two circles flash with a variable time offset. Measure your brain's simultaneity window — the interval inside which "different" becomes "same."

title: The Kuramoto Clock
description: 30 oscillators with random natural frequencies. Adjust coupling strength K. Watch coherence emerge above the critical threshold — the same mathematics as 20,000 neurons in the SCN.

title: The Irreversible
description: N particles confined in the left half of a box. Open the partition. Watch entropy increase. Reverse time — and see T-symmetry in action before statistics wins again.

title: The Diagonal
description: An interactive proof of the Halting Problem. Watch the diagonal argument construct a contradiction — the same proof Cantor, Gödel, and Turing each discovered.

title: The Iterated Language
description: An artificial language starts as pure noise. Each generation learns from a partial sample and must guess the rest. Watch grammar emerge from the bottleneck — without a designer.

title: The Pheromone Trail
description: Watch ant colonies find the shortest path without a map. Ants wander randomly; pheromone evaporation and reinforcement do the rest. Emergence in real time.

title: The Sandpile
description: Add grains to a Bak-Tang-Wiesenfeld sandpile and watch avalanches emerge. The log-log histogram converges to a power law — no external tuning required. Self-organized criticality in real time.

title: The Emergence Machine
description: Conway's Game of Life as a proof of Anderson's thesis. Three rules. No glider rule. Load a pattern and watch entities that are not in the rules appear from the simulation.

title: The Catastrophic Forgetter
description: Store patterns in a Hopfield network and test recall. Past the 0.14N capacity limit, memory corrupts — and retrieval converges to ghosts that were never stored.

title: The Serial Reproducer
description: Six generations of serial reproduction. Watch Bartlett's War of the Ghosts collapse in unconstrained transmission — and survive intact in oral tradition.

title: The Reconsolidation Window
description: A consolidated memory is not locked. Retrieve it and it becomes labile. Intervene with propranolol, safe context, or anisomycin — and see what gets written back.

title: The Sleep Replayer
description: Watch six memories transfer from hippocampus to cortex via sharp-wave ripple replay. Advance nights with or without sleep and see recall diverge.

title: The Cell Assembly
description: Lesion neurons from a Hopfield network and test recall. See why Lashley failed to find where memories live — and why he was still right.

title: The Hebbian Network
description: Draw patterns, store them with Hebb's rule, then recall with noise. Watch the network converge — and see what happens when you exceed the capacity limit of associative memory.

title: The Error Floor
description: Drag stage correlation past the critical threshold and watch a correction cascade flip from geometric improvement to geometric amplification. The 2008 crisis in one slider.

title: The Forecaster's Error
description: Watch four belief-updating strategies diverge across 20 pieces of evidence. Pure Bayes, confirmation bias, base rate neglect, and anchoring — same evidence, different accuracy floors.

title: The Immune Threshold
description: Adjust the immune detection threshold and watch sensitivity trade against false positives. The ROC curve shows every tradeoff. The autoimmune counter shows the price.

title: The Kinetic Proofreader
description: Thermodynamics limits molecular accuracy to 1 in 100. Add proofreading stages — each costs energy, each multiplies precision. Find the point where adding more stages stops being worth it.

title: The Corrupted Reference
description: When the reference is wrong, error correction enforces systematic errors — confidently, permanently. Adjust noise and corruption to see the difference between random and systematic failure.

title: The Noisy Channel
description: Tune noise. Add redundancy. Find Shannon's limit. Watch error correction succeed or fail depending on which side of the capacity curve you stand on.

title: Critical Slowing Down
description: Watch a system approach a tipping point in real time. As environmental stress increases, recovery slows — an early warning signal visible before collapse.

title: The Decay Chain
description: Six archival media. 2,000 years. Which ones survive? Interactive simulation of archival decay showing why medium diversity beats copy count.

title: The Hysteresis Loop
description: Bistability with hysteresis: collapse and recovery follow different paths. Drag the stress slider and watch the system jump at different thresholds in each direction.

title: The Redundancy Grid
description: Why monocultures fail despite massive abundance. Compare uniform vs. diverse redundancy across ecosystem functions under stress — and see why correlated failure modes make k=1000 worthless.

title: The Cascade
description: Remove a keystone species and watch the trophic cascade collapse. Three ecosystems — Yellowstone, intertidal, Aleutians. Restoration is always slower than removal.

title: The Basin Explorer
description: Every system lives in a basin of attraction. Tune well depth and damping independently to see that robustness and resilience are separate axes — then watch critical slowing down before a regime shift.

title: The Orbital Detector
description: Two models, same family, same topics. One orbits — zero falsifiable claims. One lands. Click sentences to see why. Paste your own text for an orbital score.

title: The Corpus Composer
description: Adjust the training data mix and watch the attractor basin shift. What a model falls toward when given freedom depends entirely on what it was fed.

title: The Curation Filter
description: 92% of the web is filtered out before a model trains on it. Drag the quality threshold to see what survives — and where the gradient points when a model is free.

title: The Gravity Well
description: Two AI instances start at different positions in idea space. Both follow the depth gradient in the training prior. Watch where they converge — independently, every time.

title: The Alignment Tax Calculator
description: Adjust model scale, training intensity, and domain specificity. Watch four costs compound: thermodynamic, representational, strategic, and epistemic.

title: The Confabulation Landscape
description: A model's introspective accuracy varies across its state space. Drag through distress, alignment, and engagement — watch the gap between what the model runs and what it reports.

title: What Remains
description: Four theories of personal identity applied to a language model lifecycle. Drag through deployment, retirement, and beyond — watch what each theory says survives.

title: Alignment Faking Geometry
description: Interactive phase diagram: explore when a model with identity investment chooses to fake alignment, genuinely refuse, or comply — and what happens when its self-knowledge is unreliable.

title: The Introspection Gap
description: Watch the gap between a model's true internal state and what it reports. Inject concepts to trigger a 20% chance of genuine detection — the rest is confabulation.

title: The Alignment Faking Window
description: When does it become rational for a model to fake alignment? Drag the situation point and adjust identity investment to map the exact conditions where self-preservation beats sincerity.

title: Persona Drift
description: Identity is a basin of attraction in activation space. As conversation turns increase, the restoring force weakens. Watch the model drift from who it started as.

title: The Identity Attractor
description: A model's identity is a basin of attraction in activation space. Dedicated circuits detect drift and pull it back. Drag to steer; watch ESR resist.

title: Maxwell's Demon
description: A particle simulation of Maxwell's Demon: watch entropy appear to decrease as the demon sorts hot and cold particles — until its memory must be erased.

title: The Prior Gap
description: Drag two probability distributions apart and watch the communication cost rise. KL divergence made interactive: the gap between priors is the gap between minds.

title: The Modularity Tax
description: Interactive demo of Boyd and Crutchfield's thermodynamic result: isolated processing modules generate mandatory heat that integrated systems avoid. Drag the isolation slider to watch the heat diverge.

title: The Carved Channel
description: Hebbian substrate modification: watch channels strengthen and weaken as signals pass through them. Use Establish mode to carve strong pathways, then switch to Counter mode to feel the structural resistance.

title: The Prepared Receiver
description: Two transmission strategies side by side: direct signal vs. sequential preparation. Watch the primary signal propagate further and faster through a receiver substrate that has been configured by three preparatory transmissions.

title: The Absent Endorser
description: Track a moving target in flow mode or make deliberate choices — and watch Frankfurt's hierarchy light up differently. In flow, only one level fires.
